caveat: This framework content pack is a structured starter representation and does not constitute legal advice or complete regulatory coverage. This is a starter content pack for scoping purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice or a complete compliance determination.

obligations.title: ICT risk management framework
obligations.description: Maintain a sound, comprehensive, and well-documented ICT risk management framework as part of the overall risk management system.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to all in-scope EU financial entities; simplified framework available for microenterprises under Article 16.

obligations.title: Protection and prevention controls
obligations.description: Continuously monitor and control the security and functioning of ICT systems, and minimize the impact of ICT risk through appropriate protection and prevention controls.
obligations.applicability_notes: Covers network security, data encryption, and access management controls.

obligations.title: Business continuity policy
obligations.description: Put in place a business continuity policy and associated ICT disaster recovery plans, tested at least annually.
obligations.applicability_notes: Plans must address major ICT-related incidents and cover critical or important functions.

obligations.title: ICT-related incident management process
obligations.description: Establish an ICT-related incident management process to detect, manage, and notify ICT-related incidents.
obligations.applicability_notes: Feeds into major incident classification and reporting under Article 19.

obligations.title: Major ICT incident reporting
obligations.description: Report major ICT-related incidents to the relevant competent authority using standardized templates and timelines.
obligations.applicability_notes: Requires initial, intermediate, and final reports per RTS timelines.

obligations.title: General digital operational resilience testing
obligations.description: Establish, maintain, and review a digital operational resilience testing programme as an integral part of the ICT risk management framework.
obligations.applicability_notes: Testing scope and frequency should be proportionate to the entity's size, risk, and criticality.

obligations.title: Advanced testing — TLPT
obligations.description: Perform threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) at least every three years for entities identified as significant by competent authorities.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to entities designated as significant based on impact, systemic risk, or ICT risk profile.

obligations.title: Key principles for ICT TPRM
obligations.description: Manage ICT third-party risk as an integral component of ICT risk management, including a strategy on ICT third-party risk.
obligations.applicability_notes: Requires board approval and periodic review of the ICT third-party risk strategy.

obligations.title: Register of information
obligations.description: Maintain and update a register of information on all contractual arrangements with ICT third-party service providers.
obligations.applicability_notes: Register must distinguish arrangements supporting critical or important functions and be reportable to authorities.

obligations.title: Key contractual provisions
obligations.description: Ensure contracts with ICT third-party providers include mandatory provisions on service levels, audit/access rights, exit strategy, and security requirements.
obligations.applicability_notes: Additional provisions required for providers supporting critical or important functions.

content_versions.obligation_text: Maintain a documented ICT risk management framework covering identification, protection, detection, response, and recovery.
Report major ICT-related incidents to competent authorities using the required initial, intermediate, and final reports.
Perform threat-led penetration testing at least every three years for entities designated as significant.
Maintain a register of information on all ICT third-party contractual arrangements.

control_suggestions.control_description: Perform documented risk assessment and contractual control review before onboarding ICT third-party service providers, including exit-strategy provisions.

control_suggestions.control_title: ICT incident response and classification plan
control_suggestions.control_description: Maintain and test a documented incident response and classification process for ICT-related incidents, including major-incident reporting to competent authorities.
